# Cycle 100 ‚ÄĒ Subgroup Elemsym Moment Character Sum

**Classification: BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL.**

I do **not** prove the cancellation bound and I do **not** produce an aperiodic counterpacket. I bank an exact equivalence that shows the bound is *not a relaxation* of anything, then I expose the decisive structural fact that the literal character sum targets a **fiber-weighted** count `N` strictly larger than the prize numerator `|őė_U|`. This splits Cycle100 into two genuinely separate sublemmas, cuts the termwise/Weil approach, makes the periodic-charging mechanism explicit, and proves the fiber bound in the structured (reserve-capped) cases.

## 0. Setup (not re-deriving Cycle98/99)

`p` prime, `n|p‚ąí1`, `H=őľ_n‚Č§F_p^*`, `‚ąŹ_{x‚ąąH}(X‚ąíx)=X^n‚ąí1`. `U` monic, `deg U=s+1`, `s=k+ŌÉ`, `k=ŌĀn`, reserve `ŌÉ‚Č•Cn/\log n`. `P=(P_1,‚Ä¶,P_{ŌÉ+1})` the power-sum prefix of `U`. `Ōą` a nontrivial additive character. `L_t(X)=‚ąĎ_{j=1}^{ŌÉ+1}t_jX^j`, `E_s(t)=e_s(\{Ōą(L_t(x))\}_{x‚ąąH})`, `S(t)=‚ąĎ_{őł‚ąČH}Ōą(L_t(őł))`. Goal numerator: `őė_U=\{őł‚ąąF_p‚ąĖH : v(őł)‚ąąP‚ąíM_s\}`, target `|őė_U|‚Č§n^{O(1)}`.

## 1. The reduction is an exact identity, not a bound (bankable)

Let `F(őł)=\#\{S‚äÜH,|S|=s : p_j(S)=P_j‚ąíőł^j,\ j‚Č§ŌÉ+1\}` and `N=‚ąĎ_{őł‚ąČH}F(őł)`. Expanding the indicator `1[p_j(S)=c_j‚ąÄj]=p^{-(ŌÉ+1)}‚ąĎ_tŌą(‚ąĎ_jt_j(p_j(S)‚ąíc_j))` with `c_j=P_j‚ąíőł^j`, using `‚ąĎ_{x‚ąąS}L_t(x)=‚ąĎ_jt_jp_j(S)` and `‚ąĎ_{|S|=s}‚ąŹ_{x‚ąąS}Ōą(L_t(x))=E_s(t)`:

```
N = p^{-(ŌÉ+1)} ő£_t Ōą(-<t,P>) E_s(t) S(t).
```

The `t=0` term is `E_s(0)=C(n,s)`, `S(0)=p‚ąín`, so with `main:=C(n,s)(p‚ąín)/p^{ŌÉ+1}`,

```
ő£ := ő£_{t‚Č†0} Ōą(-<t,P>) E_s(t) S(t)  =  p^{ŌÉ+1}( N ‚ąí main ).
```

`main‚Č§2^n/(n+1)^ŌÉ‚Č§2^{n(1‚ąíC/\ln 2)}‚Č§1` for `C‚Č•\ln 2` (side condition, holds in the intended reserve). Since `N‚ąą‚Ą§_{‚Č•0}`:

> **Lemma 1 (exact equivalence).** `|ő£| ‚Č§ p^{ŌÉ+1}n^{O(1)} ‚üļ N ‚Č§ n^{O(1)}`.

This is an **identity**, so the character-sum form gives *no slack*: proving the boxed bound is *exactly* as hard as the combinatorial statement `N‚Č§n^{O(1)}`. In particular:

> **Route-cut 1 (no pointwise estimate can work).** Any termwise/Weil/Gauss-sum bound is hopeless. Weil gives `|S(t)|‚Č§ŌÉ‚ąöp+n`, but the only available bound on the symmetric transform is the trivial `|E_s(t)|‚Č§C(n,s)`, so the termwise bound is `|ő£|‚Č§p^{ŌÉ+1}¬∑C(n,s)¬∑(ŌÉ‚ąöp+n)` ‚ÄĒ off the target by the full factor `C(n,s)¬∑‚ąöp`. The content lives entirely in the `t`-average, i.e. in `N` itself. Weil controls only `S(t)`; it never touches `E_s(t)`, which is the obstruction.

## 2. The decisive observation: `N` is fiber-weighted, `|őė_U|` is support (ROUTE_CUT)

`N=‚ąĎ_{őł‚ąČH}F(őł)` counts **pairs** `(őł,S)`. The prize numerator is the **support** `|őė_U|=\#\{őł:F(őł)‚Č•1\}`. Writing `F_max:=\max_{c‚ąąF_p^{ŌÉ+1}}\#\{S‚äÜH,|S|=s:p_{‚Č§ŌÉ+1}(S)=c\}`:

> **Lemma 2 (split).** `N‚Č§n^{O(1)} ‚üļ ( |őė_U|‚Č§n^{O(1)} ) ‚ąß ( F_max‚Č§n^{O(1)} )`.
> Proof: `N=‚ąĎ_{őł‚ąąőė_U}F(őł)` gives `\max(|őė_U|,F_max)‚Č§N‚Č§|őė_U|¬∑F_max`. ‚ąé

So the literal character-sum bound silently bundles a **fiber-multiplicity bound `F_max‚Č§n^{O(1)}`** that the prize goal does *not* require. `F_max‚Č§poly` is **not** implied by `|őė_U|‚Č§poly`: a single external `őł` can have many `s`-subsets `S` with `S‚ą™\{őł\}` carrying the prescribed prefix (`S_1,S_2` are Prouhet‚ÄďTarry‚ÄďEscott-equivalent on `őľ_n` to order `ŌÉ+1`). Hence:

> **Route-cut 2.** If `F_max` is superpolynomial for some aperiodic `P`, the boxed character-sum bound is **false** even though the prize target `|őė_U|‚Č§n^{O(1)}` may hold. The character sum as written is the **wrong object**: it weights each active root by its PTE-fiber. The correct target is the **distinct-point line incidence** of Cycle99's Lemma B (`őł` is recovered as `őł=e_1(S')+W_1`, so distinct points ‚ÜĒ distinct `őł`), which deflates fibers automatically. To salvage the character-sum route one must separately prove the fiber bond `F_max‚Č§n^{O(1)}`.

The support count is **not** itself a clean character sum: `1[F(őł)‚Č•1]` is non-linear; `1[F(őł)‚Č•1]‚Č§F(őł)` recovers `N`, and the reverse needs `F_max`. This is precisely why the route cannot avoid `F_max`.

## 3. Which `t` resonate, and how the periodic core is charged (bankable)

`|E_s(t)|=C(n,s)` only at `t=0`. `E_s(t)` is *large* exactly when `\{Ōą(L_t(x))\}_{x‚ąąH}` is concentrated, i.e. when `L_t` factors through a quotient `H‚Ü†H/őľ_M=őľ_{n/M}` (`M|n`, `M>1`). Since `x‚Ü¶x^j` is constant on `őľ_M`-cosets iff `M|j`:

> **Resonant frequency support.** `L_t` descends to `őľ_{n/M}` ‚üļ `t` is supported on `M‚Ą§` (`t_j=0` for `M‚ą§j`). These are the only `t` without symmetric-function cancellation.

The phase `Ōą(-<t,P>)` pairs these with the projection of `P` onto coordinates `M‚Ą§`. **`P` aperiodic** (not supported on any `M‚Ą§`, `M|n`, `M>1`) is exactly the hypothesis that dephases the resonant `t`.

> **Charging the quotient-periodic core.** On resonant `t` (supported on `M‚Ą§`), set `\tilde L(y)=‚ąĎ_i t_{Mi}y^i` (`deg‚Č§‚Ćä(ŌÉ+1)/M‚Ćč`), so `L_t(x)=\tilde L(x^M)`. Then `\{Ōą(L_t(x))\}_{x‚ąąőľ_n}` is the `M`-fold repetition of `\{Ōą(\tilde L(y))\}_{y‚ąąőľ_{n/M}}`, and `S(t)=‚ąĎ_{őł‚ąČőľ_n}Ōą(\tilde L(őł^M))`. The resonant block is **the same problem one level down**: subgroup `őľ_{n/M}`, reserve `ŌÉ'=‚Ćä(ŌÉ+1)/M‚Ćč‚ąí1‚ČąŌÉ/M`, same field. Recursing terminates at the aperiodic core. The combinatorial witness (union of `őľ_M`-cosets) auto-satisfies the prefix only when `M>ŌÉ+1`, which forces `n/M<\log n`, capping that contribution at `2^{\log n}=n^{O(1)}`.

## 4. Partial proof of the fiber bound `F_max ‚Č§ n^{O(1)}` (new, unconditional in structured cases)

> **Lemma 3 (PTE min-distance).** Distinct `S_1,S_2` in any fiber satisfy `|S_1‚Ė≥S_2|‚Č•2(ŌÉ+2)`.
> Proof: equal `e_{‚Č§ŌÉ+1}` gives `‚ąŹ_{A}(1‚ąíxX)‚Č°‚ąŹ_{B}(1‚ąíxX)\pmod{X^{ŌÉ+2}}` for `A=S_1‚ąĖS_2,B=S_2‚ąĖS_1`, `|A|=|B|=w`. The difference has degree `‚Č§w`; if `w‚Č§ŌÉ+1` it vanishes, forcing `A=B=‚ąÖ`. So `w‚Č•ŌÉ+2`. (Equivalently: any nonzero `c‚ąą\{‚ąĎc_xx^j=0,\,j‚Č§ŌÉ+1\}` has Hamming weight `‚Č•ŌÉ+2` by Vandermonde.) ‚ąé

> **Lemma 4 (reserve cap on independent flips).** Any fiber generated from a base `S_0` by pairwise-disjoint balanced PTE swaps `(A_i,B_i)` has size `‚Č§2^{\,n/(2(ŌÉ+2))}=2^{O(\log n)}=n^{O(1)}`, since each swap consumes `‚Č•2(ŌÉ+2)` distinct points and `ŌÉ+2‚Č•Cn/\log n`. ‚ąé

All structured large fibers I can build ‚ÄĒ coset-pair swaps `(ő≥őľ_M,ő≥'őľ_M)` with `M>ŌÉ+1`, nested-subgroup swaps, periodic patterns ‚ÄĒ are of this disjoint type and are capped at `n^{O(1)}` by Lemma 4, **even when the common prefix is aperiodic** (the periodicity hides in the swaps, not the prefix). This is strong evidence that `F_max‚Č§n^{O(1)}` is **true** and that the reserve `ŌÉ‚Čąn/\log n` is exactly the threshold that enforces it. The open case is fibers built from *overlapping* PTE patterns, where min-distance + packing only yields exponential bounds.

## 5. Exact next lemmas (PLAN ‚Üí PROOF)

Cycle100 cleanly resolves into:

- **L1 (subgroup PTE-multiplicity / fiber bound).** `F_max=\max_c \#\{S‚äÜH,|S|=s:p_{‚Č§ŌÉ+1}(S)=c\}‚Č§n^{O(1)}` for aperiodic `c`, after periodic charging. Status: proved for disjoint/structured fibers (¬ß4); open for overlapping null-patterns. This is the *smaller, more tractable* target and is where I have real partial progress.
- **L2 (support / line incidence).** `|őė_U|=\#(‚Ąď‚ą©E_{\le ŌÉ+1}(\binom{H}{m}))_{\text{distinct}}‚Č§n^{O(1)}` ‚ÄĒ the prize numerator; sub-Johnson list decoding on a multiplicative subgroup for a structured received word. The dominant remaining wall.
- **Single-frequency seed.** Bound `e_s(\{Ōą(c\,x^j)\}_{x‚ąąH})` for one monomial `L_t=c\,x^j` (a Gauss-sum symmetric function); ladder to the full `(ŌÉ+1)`-frequency pencil with aperiodicity removing the `M‚Ą§`-support.

`N‚Č§n^{O(1)} ‚üļ L1‚ąßL2`. **Proving L1‚ąßL2 ‚üĻ PROOF** of the boxed bound and of Cycle99. A superpolynomial overlapping fiber for aperiodic `P` ‚üĻ **COUNTERPACKET to the bound as stated** (but not to the prize goal ‚ÄĒ see ¬ß2).

## 6. Checker spec (for Codex)

Extend `cycle99_divisor_line_incidence_check.py`. On small `(p,n,k,ŌÉ)`: (i) recompute `N` two ways ‚ÄĒ direct pair count vs. `p^{-(ŌÉ+1)}‚ąĎ_tŌą(-<t,P>)E_s(t)S(t)` ‚ÄĒ and assert equality and `ő£=p^{ŌÉ+1}(N‚ąímain)`; (ii) compute `F_max`, `|őė_U|`, and verify `\max(|őė_U|,F_max)‚Č§N‚Č§|őė_U|¬∑F_max`; (iii) sweep `P` periodic vs aperiodic; (iv) for each fiber, record `\min|S_1‚Ė≥S_2|` (assert `‚Č•2(ŌÉ+2)`) and whether it is disjoint-swap-generated. Certificate: `{p,n,k,sigma,s, aperiodic, N, main, support=|őė_U|, F_max, min_symdiff, johnson_gap=s¬≤‚ąíkn, status}`. **Counterpacket trigger:** aperiodic family with `F_max/n^c‚Üí‚ąě` (kills the literal bound) or `|őė_U|/n^c‚Üí‚ąě` (kills the prize goal).

## Self-audit

1. **Proved:** the exact identity `ő£=p^{ŌÉ+1}(N‚ąímain)` with `main‚Č§1` (so the boxed bound ‚üļ `N‚Č§n^{O(1)}`, an *identity* ‚ÄĒ Lemma 1); the split `N‚Č§poly ‚üļ |őė_U|‚Č§poly ‚ąß F_max‚Č§poly` (Lemma 2); PTE min-distance `‚Č•2(ŌÉ+2)` (Lemma 3); the reserve cap `n^{O(1)}` on disjoint-swap fibers (Lemma 4); the resonant-`t`-support characterization and the periodic descent. **Not proved:** the boxed cancellation bound itself; L2 (`|őė_U|‚Č§poly`); L1 in the overlapping case. So **not** the polynomial bound in the live range.
2. **Research/model + bankable reduction**, not an official-prize theorem. (Even the boxed bound, if proven, only feeds the upper/safe side; it is not itself a prize statement.)
3. **First failure line:** the boxed bound targets the **fiber-weighted `N`, not the support `|őė_U|`** (¬ß2). If `F_max` is superpolynomial for aperiodic `P` (overlapping PTE patterns), the bound is *false as stated* while the prize goal can still hold. This is the exact line where the route, not the theorem, breaks.
4. **Ledgers:** single field, `p=q_gen=q_line=q_code=q_chal`, `n|p‚ąí1`, `k=ŌĀn`, reserve `ŌÉ‚Č•Cn/\log n`; the `2^{-128}` target ‚ÜĒ requiring a polynomial list/support size. Used consistently; `main‚Č§1` needs `C‚Č•\ln 2`, flagged.
5. **Yes, decisively.** Quotient/periodic structure produces the resonant `t` (support `M‚Ą§`) and PTE swaps, which (a) need charging and (b) **inflate `N` above `|őė_U|` via fibers** ‚ÄĒ the central reduction of the claimed numerator. Same-slope/contained collisions: `őł‚Ü¶r` injective (Cycle99) so distinct points ‚ÜĒ distinct `őł`; the inflation is purely the fiber `F(őł)`, capped to `n^{O(1)}` in all structured cases (¬ß4). Affine color normalization does not further reduce the numerator here.
6. **PLAN‚ÜíPROOF:** prove L1 (subgroup PTE-multiplicity, overlapping case) and L2 (sub-Johnson subgroup-RS support bound); together they give `N‚Č§poly` and the boxed bound. **PLAN‚ÜíCOUNTERPACKET:** an aperiodic `P` with a superpolynomial overlapping fiber (kills the boxed bound) ‚ÄĒ but the prize-relevant falsifier must instead make the *support* `|őė_U|` superpolynomial.

**Route to a full solve?** Yes, conditional on **L2** (the real wall) plus **L1** (now the cleaner secondary target, with ¬ß4 as genuine partial progress). The single sharpest recommendation: **stop bounding `N`; bound the distinct-point line incidence `|őė_U|` directly** (Cycle99 Lemma B), and treat `F_max‚Č§n^{O(1)}` as a separate, reserve-capped PTE-multiplicity lemma rather than smuggling it inside the character sum. The next exact object is the single-monomial slice `e_s(\{Ōą(c\,x^j)\}_{x‚ąąH})`, building to the aperiodic `(ŌÉ+1)`-frequency pencil.
